name: Nonmyeloablative HSCT relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis RCT
summary: A randomized clinical trial comparing nonmyeloablative hematopoietic stem cell transplantation with continued disease-modifying therapy in relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis.
population: People with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis and recent relapses despite disease-modifying therapy

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Stem Cell Replacement and Transplantation because they can show whether the intervention changes function and safety in People with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis and recent relapses despite disease-modifying therapy. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
